title: Normalize structured lifecycle/state failures into machine-readable recovery guidance
summary: Define and implement a consistent structured failure contract for lifecycle- and state-related MCP tool failures so that AI agents can branch on canonical recovery guidance without relying only on human-readable error strings. This ticket focuses on begin/resume conflicts, terminal-state violations, file-intent ordering failures, verify/commit precondition failures, and integrity-blocked state outcomes, while preserving strict canonical lifecycle validation.
outputs: A standardized machine-readable failure response shape for lifecycle- and state-related tool failures
Stable error-code categories for common workflow and canonical-state failure modes
Structured failure responses for begin-required, resume-required, terminal, file-intent, verify/commit precondition, and integrity-blocked cases
Regression tests that verify agents can branch on structured failure fields instead of string-only failure text
acceptance_criteria: Lifecycle- and state-related failures return structured failure payloads instead of relying only on human-readable exceptions or free-form strings.
Structured failures include at least `ok: false`, `error_code`, `reason`, `recoverable`, `recommended_next_tool`, and `recommended_action` when applicable.
When known, structured failures also expose canonical guidance fields such as `canonical_status`, `canonical_phase`, `next_action`, `terminal`, `active_tx_id`, and `active_ticket_id`.
Agents can distinguish begin-required, resume-required, terminal, integrity-blocked, and invalid-ordering failures from structured fields without depending only on string matching.
Integrity-related failures preserve rebuild metadata such as `blocked`, `rebuild_warning`, `rebuild_invalid_seq`, and `rebuild_observed_mismatch` where applicable.
The implementation preserves strict lifecycle validation and does not weaken canonical invariants merely to produce friendlier failure results.
tasks: Catalog the main lifecycle/state failure families currently exposed by wrapper tools, helper tools, state store validation, and replay/integrity handling.

Define stable error-code names for begin-required, resume-required, event-after-terminal, terminal-verification, duplicate-file-intent, missing-file-intent, verify-pass prerequisite, commit prerequisite, and integrity-blocked failure categories.
Add or refactor shared helpers so structured failure responses can be built consistently from canonical state and known recovery semantics.
Update lifecycle wrapper tools and helper tools so they return structured failure payloads with machine-readable guidance where canonical state is known.
Ensure integrity-blocked failures preserve diagnostic metadata and clearly indicate that unsafe canonical progression is blocked until repair or replacement of invalid history.
Add regression tests that assert the presence and semantics of structured failure fields across representative lifecycle/state failure paths.
notes: This ticket should preserve existing strictness while making failure semantics more operable for AI agents and client workflows.
Where backward compatibility matters, legacy human-readable error text may remain, but structured fields should become the authoritative branching surface for agent behavior.
Failure normalization should be driven from canonical state whenever possible rather than from ad hoc local inference.
The ticket should explicitly cover the difference between `tx.begin required`, `resume the active transaction`, and `event after terminal`, because those are common agent decision points.
Integrity-blocked failures should remain explicit and should never be represented as healthy resumable state.
